ai_analysis.root_cause: Database instance prod-database-1 was manually upgraded from db.t3.large to db.r5.2xlarge at 2:15 PM by john.doe@company.com. CloudWatch metrics show the instance was significantly over-provisioned with average CPU at 15% and max at 28%, suggesting the upgrade was unnecessary.

remediation_steps.action: Review the reason for the instance upgrade with john.doe@company.com
Revert to db.t3.large if the upgrade was not required for upcoming workload
Implement approval workflow for production database changes >$100/month
Enable RDS Performance Insights to monitor actual utilization before future upgrades

ai_analysis.executive_summary: An unnecessary database upgrade increased costs by 150%. The instance was over-provisioned with low CPU utilization (15% average). Reverting the change would save $3,240 annually with no performance impact.
ai_analysis.cloudformation_remediation:
# Revert RDS instance to previous configuration
Resources:
  ProdDatabase:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      DBInstanceIdentifier: prod-database-1
      DBInstanceClass: db.t3.large
      # Estimated savings: $270/month

historical_context.pattern: This is the third cost spike for this RDS instance in 60 days, suggesting a need for better change management controls.
